# Transcrição: 08-ACTIVITY- Creating the Frame of Your Professional or Business Story

- **Fonte:** 08-ACTIVITY- Creating the Frame of Your Professional or Business Story
- **Duração:** 01:47
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:47]

Excellent. I love the look of that thesis statement. Nice job. Well done. Now, you're simply going to do the same thing for your professional story. It might be a little more challenging. When we tell personal stories, we are deeply attached to them because that stuff happened to us and we feel it. When we tell a professional story, sometimes we're speaking about not ourselves but our organizations. We might be talking about our products or our services. We don't have the emotional connections that we typically have to a personal story, but that doesn't mean you can't do it. You want to find your five-second moment for your professional story. Maybe it is, this is the product we have to offer you today. What's the opposite of that? This was the product we wanted to build one day and put that in a thesis statement. We once were a company who wanted to build X and then some stuff happened and we built X.3. Not quite what we wanted, maybe even better. Or we once were a company that didn't know where we were going. We started with this idea and then some stuff happened and now we are this company doing this amazing work. Whatever it is, you have to have a thesis statement for the professional story too. It's going to be a little harder. It's going to be a little less clear. It may require more sentences than that personal story, but you've got to have it. You've got to have the roadmap. You've got to have the beginning and the end. They have to be in conversation with each other. They have to be approximately opposite of each other. That will help you figure out how to tell the story. Go ahead now, put some thought into it. Come up with the thesis statement for that professional story too. Go for it.
